title: HIPAA and AI: what every medical professional needs to know
post:
If you're a medical professional using AI and you're not thinking about HIPAA, you're playing with fire. Patient data privacy isn't optional — it's the foundation of trust that the entire healthcare system runs on.

Here's the basics you need: any AI tool handling patient data needs to be HIPAA-compliant, which means business associate agreements, data encryption, and audit trails. Many popular AI tools are not designed for healthcare use and don't meet these requirements. The convenience of using a free AI tool isn't worth a HIPAA violation and the massive fines that come with it.

Ask your compliance officer. Read the terms of service. Make sure you're protected before you upload a single patient record.

What's your approach to AI compliance in healthcare? Thoughts?
